THE GAZETTE OF INDIA: EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(i)] ^ W % * i r o * i M n > ^ 3 , T ^ ( i ) 3 H « M f t M f r H ^ (1) ffl.^lA 32 (3T) ?TKhl 20-1-2005 ( 2 ) W A 35 (-3T) fllfar 20-1-2005 (3) m^it.f%. 79 (3T) tmhl 14-2-2005 (4) TTT.W.ft. 174 (3T) <TRft3 16-3-2005 (5) m^r.f^T. 431 (3T) fTRte 30-6-2005 MINISTRY OF HEALTH AND FAMILY WELFARE (Department of Health) NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 21 st December, 2005 .R. 733(E).—Whereas a draft of certain rules further to amend the Drugs and Cosmetics Rules, 1945 was • required by Sections 12 and 33 of the Drugs and Cosmetics Act, 1940 (23 of 1940), in the Gazette of India -, Part II, S ection 3, Sub-section (i) dated the 13th December, 2004, under the notification of the Government o f lnistry of Health and Family Welfare (Department of Health), number G.S.R. 807(E), dated the 13th December, g objections and suggestions from all persons likely to be affected thereby, before the expiry of a period of •s from the date on which copies of the Official Gazette containing the said notificaion were made available to a; Mini An< 1, whereas copies of the said Gazette were made available to the public on 14-12-2004; An*!, whereas objections and suggestions received from the public on the said draft rules have been considered by the Central Government; after consult ition with the Drug: , therefore, in exercise of the powers conferred by Sections 12 and 33 of the said Act, the Central Government >:on with the Drugs Technical Advisory Board, hereby makes the following rules further to amend the Drugs Rules, 1945, namely:— (1) These rules may be called the Drugs and Cosmetics (6th Amendment) Rules, 2005. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette, 11 the Drugs and Cosmetics Rules, 1945,— [i) inrulel22F, in sub-rule (1),'Explanation' shall be omitted; -- 2 of 3 -- [MR II—-srqs3(i)] M T O T ^ T T F f l ^ : srefPJTTJT 3 (ii) rule 122G shall be numbered as sub-rule (1) thereof, and after sub-rule (1) as so renumbered, the following shall be inserted, namely :— "(2) Application for grant or renewal of a licence for operation of Blood Bank or processing of human blood components shall be made by the Blood Bank run by the Government, Indian Red Cross Society, hospital, charitable trust or voluntary organization approved by a State/Unioft Territory Blood Transfusion Council only. Explanation.—For the purpose ofthis sub-rule, "renewal" shall include renewal of any licence issued prior to the commencement of the Drugs and Cosmetics ( Amendment) Rules, 2005." (iii) In Schedule F to the said rules, in Part XII-B, undertheheading 'J. SPECIAL REAGENTS', for the entry at serial number (5), the following shall be substituted, namely,— "(5) ELISA or Rapid or RPHA test kits for Hepatitis and HTVI and II." [F. No. X-11014/6/2004-DMS & PFA] RITATEAOTIA,Jt.Secy. Foot-note : The principal rules were published in the Official Gazette vide notificaion No. F. 28-10/45-H (1) dated 21st December, 1945 and amended in the year 2005, by the following notifications pubhshed in Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (1) of the Gazette of India, namely:— (1) GSR32(E)dated20-l-2005. (2) GSR 35(E) dated 20-1-2005. (3) GSR 79(E) dated 14-2-2005. (4) GSR 174(E) dated 16-3-2005. •' (5) GSR431(E) dated30-6-2005.
